hypothesis: If the Force Delta (U∆)—the RMS difference between conservative (−∇ĤE) and directly predicted non-conservative forces (ĤFnc) from a single NNIP—is used as a data-free uncertainty metric, then it will strongly correlate with the true direct-force error (εdirect) across equilibrium and adversarial out-of-distribution structures for diverse solids, surfaces, and molecules and will outperform ad-hoc within-family ensemble variance at identifying high-error configurations.
